name: Maltese
description: One of the oldest toy breeds, the Maltese has been a prized companion of Mediterranean nobility since ancient times, depicted in Greek pottery and mentioned in writing by Aristotle. Their single-layered pure white coat produces minimal shedding and has a silky texture resembling human hair, making the breed relatively suitable for allergy sufferers but demanding daily brushing and regular bathing to stay pristine. Despite their delicate appearance Maltese are bold, spirited, and athletic little dogs that genuinely enjoy learning tricks and competing in agility trials.

conditions_detail.name: Patellar Luxation
conditions_detail.description: Patellar luxation occurs when the kneecap slips out of its normal groove in the femur. It can be medial (inward) or lateral (outward) and ranges from grade 1 (occasional slipping) to grade 4 (permanent dislocation).
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Grade 1-2 cases are managed conservatively with weight control and physiotherapy. Grade 3-4 typically require surgical correction to deepen the patellar groove and realign the tibial crest.
prevention_tips: Breed from dogs with normal patellar scores
Avoid high-impact activities in affected puppies

conditions_detail.name: Portosystemic Shunt
conditions_detail.description: A portosystemic shunt (PSS) is an abnormal blood vessel that allows blood from the gastrointestinal tract to bypass the liver, preventing detoxification. Toxins accumulate in the bloodstream causing hepatic encephalopathy. Congenital shunts are hereditary.
symptoms: Neurological signs after eating (circling, head pressing)
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Surgical closure or attenuation of the shunt using an ameroid constrictor or cellophane band is the definitive treatment. Medical management with lactulose, antibiotics, and protein-modified diet is used pre-operatively and in non-surgical cases.
prevention_tips: Do not breed from affected dogs or close relatives

conditions_detail.name: Progressive Retinal Atrophy (PRA)
conditions_detail.description: PRA is a group of hereditary diseases causing progressive degeneration of the photoreceptor cells in the retina. It leads to night blindness first, followed by complete blindness. There is no treatment, but DNA tests exist for many forms.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: There is no effective treatment or cure for PRA. Management focuses on adapting the dog's environment to maintain quality of life as vision deteriorates.
prevention_tips: DNA test breeding stock before mating
Only breed clear or carrier-to-clear pairings

conditions_detail.name: Tracheal Collapse
conditions_detail.description: Tracheal collapse is a progressive weakening of the tracheal cartilage rings causing the trachea to flatten during breathing. It produces a characteristic honking cough and respiratory distress, particularly during excitement, eating, or heat.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Medical management includes cough suppressants, bronchodilators, and weight loss. Severe cases benefit from surgical placement of intraluminal stents or extraluminal ring prostheses.
prevention_tips: Use a harness instead of collar
